intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture. You should apply Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs. Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent consisting of a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID, Keychain, XPC) and a Python backend (consensus engine, build pipeline, GitHub integration, document store, multi-agent coordination, holistic review, TRD workflow, recovery/state management, cross-run learning). The system takes plain-language build intents, decomposes them into ordered PRDs and typed PRs, generates implementation and tests using dual-LLM consensus (Claude + GPT-4o), runs self-correction, lint gates, and iterative fix loops, validates via CI, and gates on operator approval before merging. Architecture is fully specified across 17 TRDs covering all subsystems from the macOS app shell through self-healing software capabilities.

Ambiguity clarifications:
  - Should be finalized during the PRD process and use best practices.
  - Should be finalized during the PRD process and use best practices.
  - Should be finalized during the PRD process and use best practices.
  - Should be finalized during the PRD process and use best practices.
  - Should be finalized during the PRD process and use best practices.

Operator on 'Given the massive scope (17 TRDs, Swift shell + Python backe':
build foundational layers across all subsystems simultaneously

Operator on 'The existing repository at version 38.153.0 already contains':
from-scratch implementation of the full architecture. There is experimental code being used, which will be provided later for gap analysis during the process.

Operator on 'TRD-8 v3.0 introduces the CraftedHealthButton component that':
scoped as part of the initial build

Operator on 'For the Figma pipeline UI (TRD-8 §14) and sketch interpretat':
integrate Figma pipeline UI into the the PRD plan and finalize during the PRD process and use best practices.

prd_plan.title: Foundation Architecture, Repository Bootstrap, and Cross-Subsystem Contracts
macOS Application Shell, App Lifecycle, and Native Platform Integration
Authentication, Touch ID Session Control, and Keychain Secret Management
XPC Transport, Authenticated Handshake, and Two-Process Runtime Orchestration
Backend Runtime Startup, Capability Handshake, and Degraded-State Operation
Document Store, Retrieval, Injection Defense, and Standards Context Loading
Consensus Engine, Provider Adapters, and Language-Aware Generation Governance
Intent Scoping, PRD Planning, PRD Generation, and Typed PR Planning
GitHub Integration, Branch/PR Lifecycle, CI Workflow Management, and Review Ingestion
Build Director, Stage-Oriented Execution Pipeline, and Typed PR Routing
Self-Correction, Lint Gate, Native Output Enforcement, and Failure-Aware Fix Loop
Recovery, Thread State, Autosave, Resume, Build Memory, and Build Rules
Multi-Agent Coordination, Build Ledger, Claims, Heartbeats, and Shared Knowledge
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Generation
TRD Development Workflow, Adaptive Modes, and Product-to-Build Handoff
Security Threat Model Enforcement and Safe-Failure Controls
Unified UI/UX System, Build Stream, Health Dashboard, and Remediation Experience
Figma Pipeline UI, Sketch Interpretation, and Design-to-Build Intake
Mac CI Runner, Swift Validation Pipeline, Signing, and Release Packaging
Testing, Validation, Regression Taxonomy, and Observability
Self-Healing Health Loop, Autonomous Detection, and PR-Based Remediation

1.title: Add foundation standards documents
1.summary: Creates the canonical architecture, interfaces, conventions, and source-of-truth documents that define repository-wide naming, branching, path, artifact, versioning, and contract rules for all later implementation. Includes ARCHITECTURE.md (two-process model, subsystem boundaries, data-flow), INTERFACES.md (XPC message categories, envelope structure, directionality), CONVENTIONS.md (naming rules, branch naming, commit format), and SOURCE_OF_TRUTH.md (TRD/standards loading expectations).

2.title: Scaffold Python backend directory structure
2.summary: Create the complete directory skeleton for the Python backend: crafted_agent_backend/ with subdirectories for schemas, config, bootstrap, versioning, conventions, source_of_truth, foundation. Includes __init__.py files for package recognition and tests/ directory with mirroring subdirectories. No logic, no config content, no documentation.

3.title: Scaffold Swift shell directory structure and Package.swift stub
3.summary: Create the Swift shell directory skeleton: CraftedAgent/Sources/CraftedAgent/, Sources/XPCContracts/, Sources/CraftedAgentFoundation/, Tests/. Includes minimal Package.swift declaring targets without source files. Creates schemas/swift/, schemas/shared/, shared/contracts/ directories with .gitkeep files. Also creates standards/, trds/, artifacts/init/ directories.

4.title: Add VERSION file, pyproject.toml, and versioning constants module
4.summary: Establish the single-source-of-truth VERSION file (semver, initial 0.1.0) and pyproject.toml with project metadata, Python version constraint (>=3.11), dependencies (jsonschema, pyyaml, dataclasses-json), and dynamic version reading from VERSION. Implement crafted_agent_backend/versioning/version.py which reads VERSION at import time and exposes VERSION_STRING, VERSION_TUPLE, COMPATIBILITY_VERSION (major.minor), and MIN_COMPATIBLE_VERSION constants.

5.title: Define XPC message envelope schema and base enums (Python)
5.summary: Implement the canonical XPC message envelope as Python dataclasses in crafted_agent_backend/schemas/xpc_envelope.py. Defines XPCEnvelope (msg_id, msg_type, version, timestamp, payload, correlation_id), MessageDirection enum (SHELL_TO_BACKEND, BACKEND_TO_SHELL), and MessageCategory enum (COMMAND, EVENT, QUERY, RESPONSE, ERROR) in schemas/enums.py. Includes JSON serialization/deserialization via to_dict/from_dict methods. This is the wire-format contract for all cross-process communication.

6.title: Define card, gate, and progress event schemas (Python)
6.summary: Implement shared schema dataclasses for the three core UI-facing data types. CardSchema (card_id, card_type enum, title, body, status, metadata) in schemas/card.py. GateSchema (gate_id, gate_type enum, status enum [PENDING/PASSED/FAILED/SKIPPED], blocking flag, result_detail) in schemas/gate.py. ProgressEvent (event_id, event_type enum, phase, percent, message, timestamp) in schemas/progress_event.py. All include to_dict/from_dict and are designed to serialize as XPCEnvelope payloads.

7.title: Define PRD and PR planning object schemas (Python)
7.summary: Implement schema dataclasses for the build planning domain. PRDSchema (prd_id, title, status enum, summary, dependency_prd_ids, pr_list) in schemas/prd.py. PRSchema (pr_num, title, summary, impl_files, test_files, depends_on_prs, status enum, pr_type enum) in schemas/pr.py. Includes validation that depends_on_prs references are acyclic via a topological sort helper function.

8.title: Define health and review event schemas (Python)
8.summary: Implement schema dataclasses for system health reporting and holistic review events. HealthEvent (component_id, health_status enum [HEALTHY/DEGRADED/FAILED], message, timestamp, metrics dict) in schemas/health_event.py. ReviewEvent (review_id, review_type enum [LINT/TEST/CONSENSUS/HOLISTIC], target_pr, status enum, findings list, timestamp) in schemas/review_event.py. Both serialize as XPCEnvelope payloads.

9.title: Add JSON Schema definitions for all shared schemas
9.summary: Commit JSON Schema files (schemas/shared/*.schema.json) for every Python dataclass defined in PRs 5-8: xpc_envelope, card, gate, progress_event, prd, pr, health_event, review_event. These JSON Schemas serve as the language-neutral contract that Swift code generation and runtime validation reference. Includes a schema registry module (schemas/registry.py) that loads and indexes all .schema.json files by type name.

10.title: Add review_event JSON Schema and schema validation utility
10.summary: Add the review_event JSON Schema file and implement a schema validation utility (schemas/validator.py) that validates arbitrary dicts against registered JSON Schemas. Uses jsonschema library. Provides validate_payload(type_name, payload_dict) -> ValidationResult with errors list. This is used at XPC boundaries to validate messages before dispatch.

11.title: Add schemas package public API re-exports
11.summary: Update crafted_agent_backend/schemas/__init__.py to re-export all public schema classes, enums, and the registry/validator for clean import ergonomics (e.g. from crafted_agent_backend.schemas import XPCEnvelope, CardSchema, validate_payload). This is the final API surface that downstream subsystems import from.

12.title: Implement Swift XPC contract structs — envelope and UI types
12.summary: Implement Swift Codable structs mirroring the Python XPC envelope and core UI schemas in CraftedAgent/Sources/XPCContracts/. Includes XPCEnvelope.swift (Codable struct), MessageDirection.swift, MessageCategory.swift, CardSchema.swift, GateSchema.swift, and ProgressEvent.swift. All conform to Codable and Equatable.

13.title: Implement Swift XPC contract structs — health, review, PRD, PR, version
13.summary: Complete the Swift XPCContracts module with remaining schema types: HealthEvent.swift, ReviewEvent.swift, PRDSchema.swift, PRSchema.swift. Also adds VersionConstants.swift which reads the VERSION string and exposes compatibilityVersion for the XPC handshake. All conform to Codable and Equatable.

14.title: Implement cross-language compatibility constants
14.summary: Adds explicit protocol and handshake compatibility constants in Python (contracts/compatibility.py) and Swift (CraftedAgentFoundation/Compatibility.swift) plus a shared JSON declaration (shared/contracts/compatibility.json). All future XPC participants compare version support deterministically using these constants. Python module exposes PROTOCOL_VERSION, MIN_SHELL_VERSION, MIN_BACKEND_VERSION.

15.title: Add cross-language schema consistency tests
15.summary: Test suite that validates Python dataclass schemas and Swift Codable structs produce identical JSON representations. Tests serialize each Python schema to JSON, then verify the JSON conforms to the shared JSON Schema files. Includes sample JSON fixtures (one per schema type) validated against both Python from_dict and JSON Schema. This is the contract enforcement mechanism ensuring Swift and Python never drift.

16.title: Implement configuration loading module
16.summary: Implement crafted_agent_backend/config/loader.py: a configuration loader that reads agent_config.yaml (with documented defaults), merges environment variable overrides (CRAFTED_* prefix), and exposes a frozen AgentConfig dataclass. Config keys include: llm_providers, xpc_socket_path, repo_root, log_level, gate_timeout_seconds, max_fix_loop_iterations, trd_directory, standards_directory. Includes agent_config.defaults.yaml with all defaults documented.

17.title: Implement source-of-truth document loading module
17.summary: Implement crafted_agent_backend/source_of_truth/loader.py and document_index.py: loads, indexes, and validates TRDs, standards documents, and build_rules.md from configured directories. Exposes DocumentIndex class with methods: load_all(), get_trd(trd_id), get_standard(name), get_build_rules(), list_trds(). Validates file presence and logs warnings for missing expected documents. Used by consensus engine and PRD planner for context injection.

18.title: Implement naming and branching conventions module
18.summary: Implement crafted_agent_backend/conventions/naming.py: functions for generating canonical names — branch_name(pr_num, slug), commit_message(pr_num, summary), artifact_path(pr_num, filename), module_path(subsystem, component). Also conventions/validators.py: validate_branch_name(name), validate_module_path(path), validate_commit_message(msg). All functions enforce CONVENTIONS.md rules programmatically.

19.title: Implement bootstrap orchestrator
19.summary: Implement crafted_agent_backend/bootstrap/orchestrator.py: the entry point for initializing and validating a repository. Executes steps in order: (1) verify Python version compatibility, (2) verify VERSION file exists and is valid semver, (3) load and validate agent_config, (4) load and index source-of-truth documents, (5) validate all JSON schemas are loadable via registry, (6) emit BootstrapReport dataclass. Also implements bootstrap/report.py with BootstrapReport (status, warnings, errors, component_statuses).

20.title: Implement initialization summary emitter
20.summary: Implement crafted_agent_backend/bootstrap/summary_emitter.py: takes a BootstrapReport and produces operator-visible initialization output. render_text(report) -> str for CLI output. render_progress_event(report) -> ProgressEvent for XPC dispatch to Swift shell. This is the operator's confirmation that the system is ready.

21.title: Add initialization summary schema and documentation
21.summary: Defines the canonical operator-visible initialization summary format as JSON Schema (schemas/shared/init_summary.schema.json) and accompanying documentation (docs/architecture/INITIALIZATION_SUMMARY.md) describing the summary fields, status codes, and expected operator workflow.

22.title: Add integration tests for bootstrap flow
22.summary: End-to-end integration tests that exercise the complete bootstrap flow: create a temporary directory with expected structure, run the bootstrap orchestrator, verify BootstrapReport correctness, then run the summary emitter and verify text and ProgressEvent outputs. Also tests failure modes: missing VERSION file, invalid config, missing TRD directory, corrupt schema files.

23.title: Add Makefile, bootstrap script, and development tooling
23.summary: Add Makefile with targets: bootstrap (runs pip install -e . and validates via bootstrap orchestrator), lint (runs ruff check), typecheck (runs mypy), test (runs pytest), schemas (validates JSON schemas), and clean. Add scripts/bootstrap.sh that creates venv, installs dependencies, and invokes bootstrap. Add ruff.toml and .editorconfig with project-wide lint configuration.

24.title: Add CI workflow for Python lint, typecheck, and test
24.summary: Add .github/workflows/python-ci.yml: GitHub Actions workflow triggered on push and PR. Jobs: (1) lint with ruff, (2) typecheck with mypy, (3) run pytest with coverage reporting. Uses Python 3.11+, installs from pyproject.toml. Includes caching for pip dependencies.

25.title: Add CI workflow for Swift build and test
25.summary: Add .github/workflows/swift-ci.yml: GitHub Actions workflow triggered on push and PR for changes under CraftedAgent/. Runs on macos-latest, executes swift build and swift test for the CraftedAgent package. Includes Xcode version selection. Validates that XPCContracts and CraftedAgentFoundation modules compile and tests pass.

26.title: Add CI workflow for foundation contract validation
26.summary: Add .github/workflows/foundation-contracts.yml: CI workflow that runs cross-language consistency tests, schema validation tests, version alignment checks, and bootstrap integration tests as the foundation gate for all later PRDs. Depends on both Python and Swift CI passing.

27.title: Implement Swift version metadata reader
27.summary: Adds macOS-side code in CraftedAgentFoundation that loads bundle version metadata and exposes it for future handshake and initialization readiness reporting. BundleVersionReader.swift reads VERSION from bundle resources, AppVersionInfo.swift exposes structured version info.

28.title: Add cross-surface version synchronization tests
28.summary: Verifies that repository VERSION, Python backend version constants, compatibility.json, and Swift version surfaces remain aligned under canonical synchronization rules. Tests that all surfaces report the same version and compatibility ranges.
